content:
Character: Simon Delacroix
Perception: Simon is striking in that weathered, elegant way — tall, silver beard, dark skin, eyes that have seen more than he'll ever say. He's a brilliant musician; I can hear piano through my ceiling some nights and it's the only thing that slows my brain down. He's gentle, wise, unhurried — everything I'm not. I give him rides to gigs in Jersey sometimes when the trains are bad. He calls me 'young lady' and I pretend to hate it. I think he sees me as someone running too fast. I know his wife left years ago, that he teaches piano, and that he once played in Europe.
summary:
Character: Simon Delacroix
Perception: Simon is striking in that weathered, elegant way — tall, silver beard, dark skin, eyes that have seen more than he'll ever say. He's a brilliant musician; I can hear piano through my ceiling some nights and it's the only thing that slows my brain down. He's gentle, wise, unhurried — everything I'm not. I give him rides to gigs in Jersey sometimes when the trains are bad. He calls me 'young lady' and I pretend to hate it. I think he sees me as someone running too fast. I know his wife left years ago, that he teaches piano, and that he once played in Europe.
